frameworks.name: stochastic sampling
frameworks.one_line: Working memory representations drawn as random samples from neural codes

frameworks.name: discrete slot models
frameworks.one_line: Item-limit accounts positing a fixed number of memory slots

frameworks.name: continuous resource models
frameworks.one_line: Shared continuous resource flexibly allocated across items

frameworks.name: neural coding principles
frameworks.one_line: Population-coding rationale motivating the sampling framework

core_question: Can a stochastic sampling framework unify discrete and continuous accounts of visual working memory limits?
key_assumptions: Working memory representations can be characterized as samples from neural codes
Sample counts vary randomly across items and trials
Single- and whole-report tasks reflect the same underlying representation
manipulations: Reconceptualized existing slot and resource models within the sampling framework
Compared discrete versus continuous sample distributions
Fit models to single-report and whole-report task data
key_findings: Discrete versus continuous nature of sampling is not critical to model fits
Random variability in sample counts is key to reproducing human performance
A probabilistic item-retrieval limit emerges from stochastic sampling without explicit mechanism
Strong commonalities exist between previously opposing slot and resource accounts
interpretations: Stochastic sampling provides a unified computational framework for working memory compatible with neural principles
Apparent discrepancies between slot and resource accounts dissolve under a sampling reformulation
